_metadata.title: ComplianceLint Interim Standard for EU AI Act Article 10: Data and Data Governance
_metadata.disclaimer: This is an interim standard developed by ComplianceLint based on available guidance, industry best practices, and relevant ISO standards. It is NOT an official standard. It will be superseded when CEN-CENELEC publishes the harmonized standard for Article 10 (expected Q4 2026).
basis: EU AI Act Recital 67 (legislative intent on data quality)
ISO/IEC 5259-1:2024 — Data quality for analytics and ML — Part 1: Overview
ISO/IEC 5259-2:2024 — Data quality for analytics and ML — Part 2: Data quality measures
ISO/IEC 5259-3:2024 — Data quality for analytics and ML — Part 3: Data quality management
ISO/IEC 5259-4:2024 — Data quality for analytics and ML — Part 4: Data quality process framework
ISO/IEC TR 24027:2021 — Bias in AI systems and AI aided decision making
Google Model Cards / Microsoft Datasheets for Datasets
_metadata.awaiting_standard: CEN-CENELEC harmonized standard for Art. 10 (expected Q4 2026)

requirements.title: Dataset Documentation
requirements.requirement: All training, validation, and testing datasets MUST have accompanying documentation (data card, datasheet, or equivalent).
requirements.rationale: Art. 10(2) requires documented data governance practices. Without dataset documentation, it is impossible to demonstrate compliance with data quality and governance requirements.
required_content: Licensing and legal basis for data use
acceptable_formats: data_card.md or data_card.json (Google Data Cards format)
datasheet.md or datasheet.yaml (Microsoft Datasheets for Datasets)
Equivalent structured documentation in project wiki

requirements.title: Data Pipeline Documentation
requirements.requirement: Data preparation and processing operations MUST be implemented in documented, reproducible code.
requirements.rationale: Art. 10(2)(b) requires documented 'data preparation and processing operations such as annotation, labelling, cleaning, updating, enrichment and aggregation.'
acceptable_implementations: Dedicated preprocessing scripts with inline documentation
Data pipeline frameworks (Apache Beam, Prefect, Airflow)
Jupyter notebooks with clear markdown documentation
ETL scripts with configuration files
not_acceptable: Data transformations embedded in model training code without separation

requirements.title: Bias Detection and Mitigation
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST conduct and document bias examination of training data, using appropriate statistical methods or fairness toolkits.
requirements.rationale: Art. 10(2)(f) requires 'examination in view of possible biases that are likely to affect the health and safety of persons, have a negative impact on fundamental rights, or lead to discrimination.' This cannot be satisfied without systematic bias analysis.
acceptable_implementations: fairlearn (Microsoft): demographic parity, equalized odds metrics
aif360 (IBM): comprehensive bias detection and mitigation
aequitas: group fairness audit toolkit
Google What-If Tool: visual bias exploration
Custom bias analysis with documented methodology
minimum_bias_checks: Representation analysis across protected attributes (where available)
Label distribution analysis by demographic group
Feature correlation analysis with protected attributes

requirements.title: Data Versioning and Traceability
requirements.requirement: Datasets SHOULD be versioned alongside code to ensure traceability of data origin and reproducibility of model training.
requirements.rationale: Art. 10(2)(a) requires documented 'design choices' and 'data collection processes' including 'the origin of data.' Without data versioning, it is difficult to trace which data was used for which model version.
acceptable_implementations: DVC (Data Version Control) with remote storage
LakeFS for data lake versioning
Pachyderm for data pipeline versioning
Delta Lake for versioned data tables
Git LFS for smaller datasets

requirements.title: Training/Validation/Testing Dataset Separation
requirements.requirement: The provider MUST implement and document separate training, validation, and testing datasets with explicit split ratios.
requirements.rationale: Art. 10(3) requires 'relevant, sufficiently representative, and to the best extent possible, free of errors and complete' datasets for training, validation, and testing. Proper separation is fundamental to meaningful evaluation.
acceptable_implementations: Explicit train/validation/test split with documented ratios
Stratified splitting by target variable and key features
Cross-validation with documented fold strategy
Separate data collection for test set (gold standard)
minimum_requirements: Test set held out from any model selection process

requirements.title: Continuous Learning Safeguards
requirements.requirement: For AI systems that continue to learn after deployment, the provider MUST implement safeguards against biased outputs used as input for future operations (feedback loops).
requirements.rationale: Art. 10(6) explicitly requires 'appropriate measures' to prevent feedback loop biases in systems that continue learning after deployment.
acceptable_implementations: Drift detection monitoring (data drift, concept drift, prediction drift)
Periodic revalidation against held-out test sets
Human review of model updates before deployment
Feedback loop analysis and documentation
Automated rollback mechanisms when performance degrades

scoring.compliant: R01 + R03 + R05 met, and at least 2 of R02, R04, R06 met
scoring.partial: R01 or R03 met + at least 2 others met
